objects.name: Analytic 1851
objects.description: Defender correlates a sandboxed app writing high-entropy or encoded artifacts (often in app-private or shared storage), performing decode/decompress/reassembly, then dynamically loading/execing the resulting code (DexClassLoader/JNI dlopen) or spawning a helper process. Sequence: high-entropy file writes → decode/unpack bursts → new .dex/.so/.jar creation in temp/obfuscated paths → dynamic load or shell spawn within a tight window.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:logcat
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: App UID writes new file with suspicious extension/location (.tmp, .dat, .enc, /data/data/<pkg>/files/, /sdcard/Download/) and high estimated entropy

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:logcat
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: DexClassLoader/PathClassLoader load attempt from non-standard path or recently created file

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:logcat
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Short burst of file I/O followed by JNI/dlopen of a newly created .so

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:logcat
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: SELinux AVC related to execute_no_trans/execmem after decode/unpack activity by the same app UID

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: NSM:Flow
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: TLS/HTTP download with atypical MIME (application/octet-stream, application/x-zip, application/x-gzip) followed by local decode/write

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Max interval to correlate write→decode→load stages (e.g., 5–60s depending on device performance).
Shannon entropy threshold to flag likely obfuscated blobs (e.g., ≥ 7.2).
Directories to monitor (e.g., app /files, cache, /sdcard/Download). OEMs vary.
Minimum count of small sequential writes (split payload reassembly).
Benign CDNs/hosts for large opaque downloads to reduce FPs.
Foreground/background or developer mode context to suppress test noise.
